gram_worthy_spots.name: Pike Street
gram_worthy_spots.description: A vibrant downtown street known for public art displays and street projections, including political art installations that reflect Seattle's creative activism.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Pacific Place
gram_worthy_spots.description: A downtown shopping and gathering area that serves as a central location for public demonstrations and people-watching in Seattle.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Red Square at UW
gram_worthy_spots.description: The iconic University of Washington campus plaza known for its distinctive red-colored paving and as a hub for student activities and gatherings.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Third Avenue
gram_worthy_spots.description: A downtown street featured for its public art projections and light displays that capture Seattle's activist spirit and creative community.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Federal Building area (915 2nd Avenue)
off_the_beaten_path.description: A notable downtown location that serves as a gathering point for community vigils and peaceful assemblies reflecting Seattle's civic engagement.
